definitions: Organic compounds consisting of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen, characterized by a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to a saturated carbon atom within an open-chain (aliphatic) structure.
A class of alcohols that do not contain any carbon-carbon double or triple bonds within their hydrocarbon chain.
examples: Ethanol is one of the most common saturated aliphatic alcohols.
The synthesis involved several saturated aliphatic alcohols.
Many saturated aliphatic alcohols are used as solvents.
